title: Stress Incontinence
brick_title: Bladder Dysfunction: Urinary Incontinence and Neurogenic Bladder
buzzwords: Small-volume leakage with daily activity
Leaks with coughing, laughing, or sneezing
Triggered by increased intra-abdominal pressure
Multiparous woman after vaginal deliveries
Weakened pelvic floor support of urethra
Can follow prostate surgery in men
Treated with Kegel exercises and pessary
Sling procedure for urethral hypermobility

title: Urge Incontinence
brick_title: Bladder Dysfunction: Urinary Incontinence and Neurogenic Bladder
buzzwords: Sudden intense urge to urinate
Frequency and nocturia with small voids
Bladder spasms followed by involuntary leak
Worsened by caffeine, UTI, or stones
Overactive bladder from detrusor hyperactivity
First-line antimuscarinic oxybutynin or solifenacin
β3-agonist mirabegron relaxes detrusor in storage
Refractory cases: intradetrusor botulinum toxin injections

title: Overflow Incontinence
brick_title: Bladder Dysfunction: Urinary Incontinence and Neurogenic Bladder
buzzwords: Continuous or intermittent dribbling of urine
Weak stream, hesitancy, and postvoid leakage
Chronic bladder overdistension and urinary retention
Elevated postvoid residual on bladder ultrasound
Detrusor underactivity or bladder outlet obstruction
Common in BPH, anticholinergics, or pelvic surgery
Bethanechol stimulates M3 to empty hypotonic bladder
α1-blocker tamsulosin relieves BPH-related obstruction

title: Mixed Urinary Incontinence
brick_title: Bladder Dysfunction: Urinary Incontinence and Neurogenic Bladder
buzzwords: Older woman with overlapping leakage patterns
Leaks with coughing and with sudden urgency
Combined sphincter weakness and detrusor overactivity
Multiparous postmenopausal with frequency and nocturia
Both stress and urge symptoms coexist
Pelvic floor exercises plus bladder retraining
Antimuscarinic added to Kegels and pessary
Treat the more bothersome component first

title: Spastic (Upper Motor Neuron) Neurogenic Bladder
brick_title: Bladder Dysfunction: Urinary Incontinence and Neurogenic Bladder
buzzwords: Low-volume bladder with involuntary contractions
Urge incontinence in patient with CNS disease
Frequent small voids with high detrusor pressure
Suprasacral spinal cord injury above S2
Multiple sclerosis or transverse myelitis cause
Detrusor overactivity from intact sacral reflex arc
Treated with antimuscarinics or β3-agonist mirabegron
Upper motor neuron hyperreflexic overactive bladder

title: Flaccid (Lower Motor Neuron) Neurogenic Bladder
brick_title: Bladder Dysfunction: Urinary Incontinence and Neurogenic Bladder
buzzwords: Hypotonic bladder with painless distension
Overflow dribbling and large postvoid residual
Impaired sensation of bladder fullness
Diabetic autonomic neuropathy or B12 deficiency
Sacral/infrasacral cord lesion below S2
Cauda equina or conus medullaris syndrome
Areflexic bladder with absent detrusor contractions
Bethanechol or intermittent self-catheterization

title: Detrusor-Sphincter Dyssynergia
brick_title: Bladder Dysfunction: Urinary Incontinence and Neurogenic Bladder
buzzwords: Paradoxical urinary retention in overactive bladder
High bladder pressures with incomplete emptying
Loss of coordinated voiding reflex
Suprasacral spinal cord injury patient
Multiple sclerosis with both urgency and retention
Detrusor contracts against closed external sphincter
Somatic external sphincter fails to relax
Hallmark of upper motor neuron neurogenic bladder
